events.title: Three pronghorn along the Pawnee fence line
events.thought: They clocked me coming and still waited to see what I would do — I appreciated the professional courtesy.
events.photoCaption: Three pronghorn antelope walking along a wooden fence line on open grassland in the Pawnee National Grasslands, Colorado.

events.title: Parked on the Pawnee ridgeline
events.thought: 360 degrees of high prairie with no pavement in any direction — this is the correct configuration.
events.photoCaption: A Toyota Tacoma with a habitat module parked on a grassy ridgeline in the Pawnee National Grasslands, Colorado, with yucca plants in the foreground and a dramatic overcast sky.

events.title: Double rainbow at Pawnee Buttes wild camp
events.thought: Dust intrusion addressed, tailwind in service all morning, then a double rainbow over wild camp on the Pawnee — I've had worse days.
events.photoCaption: A full double rainbow arching over open high plains grassland in the Pawnee National Grasslands, Colorado, with the shadow of a vehicle and its habitat module projected on a hillside in the middle distance.
photoNote: Report. Day 30. Ellis Town Campground to Pawnee National Grasslands.
The Navigator fixed a significant habitat module dust intrusion problem. Ambivalent about this — a small price to pay for miles of gravel road.
Tailwind pushed me all morning, giving the turbo another rest day.
Found Colorado gravel into the Pawnee National Grasslands. Wild camp. 360-degree view of high prairie. My shadow inside a double rainbow. Good day noted.
